g.text: Identical greedy workloads; the only difference is whether each decode step feeds one token (cache
on) or re-runs the whole sequence (cache off).
The same request set submitted to the scheduler with one slot (sequential) and with N slots (batched
Prefill cost grows with prompt size; decode speed stays roughly flat because each cached step only
Same greedy workload, fp32 vs dynamically quantized int8 Linear layers. Token agreement is the
fraction of positions where both variants chose the same token; quantization is lossy and this makes
The KV cache delivered a 2.61x latency improvement on this workload.
Continuous batching raised throughput 2.07x over sequential serving.
Static batching peaked at 297.8 tok/s with batch size 8.
int8 cut the checkpoint from 328 MB to 239 MB with 100% greedy token agreement.
Numbers are from a real run on the machine listed above; rerun
